name: Kwanyama
notes: Kwanyama (Oshikwanyama / Kuanyama) — Bantu language of northern Namibia and southern Angola, one of the two written standards of Ovambo. Written in the Latin script. GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: the Kwanyama orthography and phoneme inventory (including prenasalised stops, the ⟨sh⟩/⟨tsh⟩ digraphs and tone, which is not written) need documented sourcing before a letter→phoneme table can be asserted. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Latin-script Kwanyama orthography. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode kuan1247), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode kuan1247.
